title: Biochemical Measures of Neuropathy - Genotyping
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ftpos","text":"Summary:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"45ffl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9j9vp","text":"Oxidative stress is highly correlated with the metabolic changes caused by hyperglycemia. Increased levels of glucose overload mitochondria and result in the production of reactive oxygen species (ROS). In addition, the flow of excess glucose through cellular pathways decreases the cell’s normal ability to detoxify ROS. As a result, the neurons and axons of the peripheral nervous system contain increased levels of ROS and decreased antioxidant capacity. The following assays are used to measure these changes in rodent models of diabetic neuropathy. steps: Prepare a Master Mix containing the following volumes of reagents:
Into each 0.5mL thin-walled tube, pipette 23.0-μL of Master Mix.
Pipet 2.0 μL of DNA template (200 ng/μL) into thin-walled tube. Mix gently, and spin down.
Verify PCR product by separation on 2% agarose.
Store tubes at –20°C until needed. If plates will be used within a few days they may be stored at 4°C.
creator: Lili Liang
